summarization.a: The figure illustrates a step-by-step process involving RIE, digital etch, ALE, and ALD techniques.
summarization.b: The figure displays close-up transmission electron microscopy images showing the evolution of InGaAs fin structures with thickness of 43 nm
summarization.c: The figure displays close-up transmission electron microscopy images showing the evolution of InGaAs fin structures with thickness of 36 nm
summarization.d: The figure displays close-up transmission electron microscopy images showing the evolution of InGaAs fin structures with thickness of 30 nm
summarization.e: The figure displays close-up transmission electron microscopy images showing the evolution of InGaAs fin structures with thickness of 24 nm
summarization.f: The figure displays close-up transmission electron microscopy images showing the evolution of InGaAs fin structures with thickness of 19 nm where fin release is initiated
summarization.g: The figure displays close-up transmission electron microscopy images showing the evolution of InGaAs fin structures with thickness of 13 nm with fin released
summarization.h: The figure displays close-up transmission electron microscopy images showing the evolution of InGaAs fin structures with thickness of 8 nm with fin released
summarization.i: The figure displays close-up transmission electron microscopy images showing the evolution of InGaAs fin structures with thickness of 3 nm with fin released

j.question: Does the etching of InAlAs depend on the orientation?
Explain the orientation-dependent etch rate.
j.answer: These TEM samples have the wafer orientation as [001] and the fin sidewall orientation is [011]. After thermal ALE, the InAlAs [111] plane underneath InGaAs was revealed, suggesting a lower etch rate on [111] plane. This is reasonable because the {111} planes have the highest packing density. Slower etch rates have indeed been observed at higher packing density surfaces in the chemical etching of Si and GaAs by Cl2.

a.question: How does thermal ALE compare with RIE and digital etch?
a.answer:
1. The narrowest fin with Wf = 3 nm in the upper part and Wf = 4 nm in the middle is obtained with ALE. (image k)
2. A TEM image of InGaAs FinFET,  fins formed by RIE and digital etch has Wf = 5 nm. (image l)

g.question: What is fin release, when does it occur?
g.answer: For very narrow fins, the thermal ALE released the InGaAs fins by undercutting the InAlAs buffer layer due to the selectivity of thermal ALE. Fin release occurs for fin width below 20 nm.
